items.title: Per Cleaning the Glass, the Hawks had a 113.0 defensive rating with EITHER Onyeka Okongwu or Jock Landale at center last season.
items.author: Brad Rowland
items.body_excerpt:
Per Cleaning the Glass, the Hawks had a 113.0 defensive rating with EITHER Onyeka Okongwu or Jock Landale at center last season.

That equates to the 6th-best defense in the NBA.

(Contrary to popular belief, they’ve proven they can defend at a high level without a dominant 7-foot rim protector!)

items.title: Something to monitor is whether the Hawks got Jock Landale to agree to waive the implicit no-trade clause that comes along with a one-year deal like this.
items.author: Brad Rowland
items.body_excerpt:
Something to monitor is whether the Hawks got Jock Landale to agree to waive the implicit no-trade clause that comes along with a one-year deal like this.

(Candidly, I would be surprised if this wasn’t part of the agreement)

items.title: Atlanta Hawks salary cap situation after re-signing Jock Landale.
items.author: Yossi Gozlan
items.body_excerpt:
Atlanta Hawks salary cap situation after re-signing Jock Landale.

This could indicate they won't pursue a big man via trade anymore.

Their proximity to the luxury tax also makes a Jonathan Kuminga return more complicated.

items.title: Sources: The Atlanta Hawks plan to sign Jock Landale to a one-year, $14 million deal. Agents Sammy Wloszczowski and Mike Lelchitski of SIG Sports worked with Hawks officials on the deal. ESPN first to report.
